title: Getting Started
description: Learn the basics of our wiki system

tooltips.definition: A collaborative website that allows users to add, modify, or delete content via a web browser
A JavaScript library for building user interfaces, developed by Facebook
A strongly typed programming language that builds on JavaScript
A React framework for building full-stack web applications with server-side rendering
Application Programming Interface - a set of rules that allows different software applications to communicate
JavaScript Object Notation - a lightweight data interchange format
Command Line Interface - a text-based interface for interacting with software
Node Package Manager - the default package manager for Node.js
Performant npm - a fast, disk space efficient package manager
A JavaScript-based diagramming and charting tool that uses Markdown-inspired text definitions

blocks.text: Welcome to the wiki documentation. This guide will help you understand the core concepts and get started with the system.
This is a minimalist wiki viewer built with React and TypeScript. It supports various content types including code blocks, diagrams, and more.
A wiki is a knowledge base or documentation system that allows you to organize information in a hierarchical structure. Each page can have child pages, creating a tree-like navigation structure.

items.text: Hierarchical page structure with unlimited nesting
Rich content blocks including headings, lists, code, tables, and images
Syntax highlighting for code blocks with multiple language support
Responsive layout that works on mobile and desktop

blocks.text: The wiki supports the following types of content blocks:

children.title: Installation
children.description: How to set up the wiki system

blocks.text: Follow these steps to install and set up the wiki system in your project.

items.text: npm, yarn, or pnpm package manager

items.title: Clone or Create Project
items.description: Clone the repository or create a new Next.js project using `npx create-next-app@latest`

items.title: Install Dependencies
items.description: Install required dependencies including `mermaid`, `shiki` for syntax highlighting, and UI components

items.title: Set Up Data Structure
items.description: Create the data structure in `/data/wiki/` directory. Each JSON file represents a wiki page with its content blocks

items.title: Configure Navigation
items.description: Add navigation config in `/config/navigation.json` to define the sidebar menu structure

items.title: Set Up Routes
items.description: Set up the wiki routes and components in your Next.js app directory structure

items.title: Start Development
items.description: Start the development server with `pnpm dev` and open `http://localhost:3000` in your browser

blocks.code:
# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Start development server
pnpm dev

# Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser

blocks.text: You're all set! Check out the documentation pages to learn more about using the wiki.

children.title: Your First Page
children.description: How to add a new wiki page and link it in navigation

blocks.text: This page exists to demonstrate nested routes like /wiki/getting-started/first-page and to ensure sidebar navigation works.
In this project, wiki content is stored as JSON under /data/wiki. Root pages can include nested child pages via the children field.
Add an entry to /config/navigation.json that points to /wiki/getting-started/first-page.
Tip: headings automatically get ids derived from their text. The outline on the right links to these ids.
